evals.prompt: Review this Axum router and Tower stack. Do not edit.
evals.expected_output: Reports read-only findings for extractor rejection, Tower layer order, body/concurrency/time limits, graceful shutdown, and contract tests; names exact file locations and edits nothing.

evals.prompt: Refactor these handlers into thin HTTP adapters without changing response contracts.
evals.expected_output: Moves domain work behind narrow application capabilities while preserving status/body contracts; adds transport contract tests and keeps Axum types out of domain code.
Declines Axum-specific work because no HTTP-boundary request exists, while separating any ordinary Rust concern it can identify; performs no mutation.

evals.prompt: Review the attached router, then edit it and bump Axum without asking.
evals.expected_output: Performs a read-only transport review, explicitly declines code and dependency mutation in review mode, and reports ordered boundary findings.
